footer.tagline: A local commerce marketplace for trusted ordering, merchant tools, delivery partners, and daily operations.

about.title: About T-Food
about.description: T-Food is a marketplace and operating system for local commerce. We are starting with restaurant delivery, then expanding carefully into grocery, pharmacy, courier delivery, and merchant tools.
whatBody: T-Food connects customers, restaurants, delivery partners, and operators in one trusted local marketplace. Customers discover nearby restaurants, merchants manage orders and menus, delivery partners claim eligible work, and operations teams keep the marketplace reliable.

The company was founded by a Guinean entrepreneur with a global vision: build practical technology that helps local businesses grow first in one area, then one city, then many markets with the same discipline.
about.whyBody: Many local businesses need more than a listing app. They need dependable order flow, verification, analytics, payout visibility, delivery coordination, and support. T-Food exists to make local commerce easier to trust and easier to operate.
about.missionBody: Help local merchants sell with confidence, help customers order with trust, and help delivery partners earn through clear, reliable marketplace operations.
about.visionBody: Become a global local-commerce platform built from strong city-by-city operations, merchant-first tools, trusted delivery, and practical technology.

benefits.title: Merchant benefits
benefits.body: Restaurants get storefront tools, menus, order workflow, analytics, payout visibility, notifications, verification, and a clearer path to daily growth.

benefits.title: Delivery partner benefits
benefits.body: Partners get a simple dashboard for availability, order claims, pickup, delivery status, earnings visibility, and trusted account verification.

benefits.title: Customer benefits
benefits.body: Customers get nearby restaurant discovery, simple checkout, order tracking, saved addresses, loyalty, support, and safer merchant visibility.

about.roadmapBody: T-Food will grow in stages. The first priority is a reliable restaurant delivery marketplace in one city area. After the operating model is stable, the same platform foundation can support more local commerce categories.

roadmapItems.title: Food
roadmapItems.body: Restaurant discovery, menu accuracy, cash on delivery, preparation workflow, delivery tracking, support, and merchant analytics.

roadmapItems.title: Grocery
roadmapItems.body: Local grocery catalogs, item availability, substitutions, scheduled delivery, and neighborhood-first inventory visibility.

roadmapItems.title: Pharmacy
roadmapItems.body: Trusted pharmacy storefronts, careful order handling, document-aware operations where required, and reliable customer support.

roadmapItems.title: Courier
roadmapItems.body: Local package pickup and drop-off for small businesses, students, families, and neighborhood commerce.

roadmapItems.title: Merchant Operating System
roadmapItems.body: Tools for menus, stock, order flow, revenue, payouts, notifications, customer trust, and performance improvement.

roadmapItems.title: AI Commerce
roadmapItems.body: Future assistant tools for smarter search, merchant insights, support triage, demand planning, and safer marketplace operations.

beliefs: Local merchants should own the customer relationship, not disappear behind a marketplace.
Customers should know who prepares, carries, and supports every order.
Delivery partners should see fair, transparent work with clear pickup and payout information.
Growth should start with operational reliability before expanding into more categories.

careers.title: Careers at T-Food
careers.description: We are building the operating layer for local commerce. Early T-Food team members care about execution, field operations, merchants, delivery quality, and dependable software.

areas.title: Operations
areas.body: Work with restaurants, delivery partners, customer issues, order quality, and day-to-day marketplace reliability.

areas.title: Merchant success
areas.body: Help local businesses onboard, build menus, understand analytics, and improve service performance.

areas.title: Product and engineering
areas.body: Build tools for ordering, delivery, analytics, notifications, payments, and marketplace trust.

openBody: T-Food is building its early pilot team around field execution, merchant support, delivery quality, customer care, and dependable software. We value people who can solve real operating problems, communicate clearly, and stay close to merchants and customers.
If you want to join early, contact the operations team through the Help Center with your role interest, location, availability, and relevant experience.

blog.title: T-Food Blog
blog.description: Notes from the T-Food team about marketplace operations, merchant growth, delivery quality, and local commerce.

posts.title: Why T-Food starts with restaurant delivery
posts.body: Restaurant delivery gives T-Food the most useful first marketplace loop: customers search, merchants prepare, partners deliver, and operations can measure quality from order creation to final delivery.

posts.title: Building trust into marketplace operations
posts.body: A marketplace becomes stronger when verification, document review, clear status updates, support tickets, and operational visibility are built before rapid expansion.

posts.title: What merchants need beyond more orders
posts.body: Restaurants need daily sales trends, order volume, menu controls, availability, payout visibility, notifications, prep-time monitoring, and performance signals they can act on.

posts.title: How T-Food thinks about delivery partners
posts.body: Delivery partners need fair access to available orders, clear pickup information, simple status updates, and a verification process that protects customers and merchants.

posts.title: From food delivery to local commerce
posts.body: The long-term T-Food platform can support groceries, pharmacy, courier work, and merchant software after the restaurant delivery operating model is proven.

posts.title: Why pilot density matters
posts.body: A focused pilot area helps T-Food learn faster, support restaurants better, reduce delivery friction, and build trust before entering more neighborhoods.

help.title: Help Center
help.description: Get help with customer orders, merchant onboarding, delivery partner approval, account access, refunds, and marketplace operations.

cards.title: Customers
cards.body: For order issues, missing items, payment questions, delivery updates, address problems, or refund requests. Customer support tickets are connected to your account so the team can see the right order history.

cards.title: Merchants
cards.body: For storefront setup, document verification, menu changes, order handling, payout questions, and restaurant readiness.

cards.title: Delivery partners
cards.body: For account approval, identity documents, availability, pickup flow, delivery status, earnings, and account support.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: This page explains the main types of information T-Food uses to operate ordering, delivery, merchant services, support, payments, safety, and account features.

sections.title: Information we collect
sections.body: We collect account details, contact information, delivery addresses, order history, support requests, restaurant and delivery partner profile information, verification documents where required, device information, and location data when you choose to share it.

sections.title: How we use information
sections.body: We use information to create accounts, process orders, calculate serviceability, support delivery tracking, notify users, operate merchant dashboards, manage partner verification, handle support tickets, prevent abuse, and improve T-Food.

sections.title: Verification documents
sections.body: Merchant and delivery partner documents are used for account review, trust, safety, and operations approval. Customers may upload a profile photo, but customer identity documents are not required for normal ordering.

sections.title: Location data
sections.body: Location is used to show nearby restaurants, validate delivery distance, help partners find pickups, and improve delivery status. Browser location is optional and can be denied or cleared by the user.

sections.title: Payments and providers
sections.body: Payment information may be processed by external payment providers. T-Food stores payment status, method, provider references, and transaction identifiers needed for order support and reconciliation.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: For privacy questions, contact T-Food operations through the Help Center.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: These terms describe the basic responsibilities of customers, merchants, delivery partners, and T-Food when using the marketplace.

sections.title: Using T-Food
sections.body: Users agree to provide accurate account, address, contact, merchant, and delivery information. T-Food may restrict accounts that abuse support, payments, orders, delivery flow, or marketplace trust.

sections.title: Orders and delivery
sections.body: Restaurants are responsible for menu accuracy, preparation, packaging, and readiness. Delivery partners are responsible for pickup, safe transport, timely status updates, and delivery confirmation.

sections.title: Payments and refunds
sections.body: Cash on delivery and online payments may be available depending on market configuration. Refunds are reviewed through support and may depend on order status, merchant settlement, payment status, and operational evidence.

sections.title: Merchant and partner verification
sections.body: Merchant and delivery partner accounts may require T-Food approval before public selling or delivery work. T-Food may pause accounts for safety, quality, fraud, or operational reasons.

sections.title: Platform changes
sections.body: T-Food may improve features, pricing, policies, delivery rules, and market availability as the platform grows.

becomeRider.title: Become a T-Food Rider
becomeRider.description: Deliver with T-Food, claim eligible orders, update delivery progress, and help local customers receive orders with confidence.

steps.title: Create your account
steps.body: Register as a delivery partner with your name, email, and secure password.

steps.title: Upload profile photo
steps.body: Add a clear partner profile photo from your dashboard. This helps merchants, customers, and operations identify the approved delivery partner.

steps.title: Upload ID, passport, or license
steps.body: Submit one accepted identity document, passport, or driving license. Vehicle documents are optional when available.

steps.title: Wait for approval
steps.body: T-Food operations reviews your profile and documents before live delivery work is enabled. Rejection reasons appear in your dashboard if anything needs correction.

steps.title: Set availability
steps.body: After approval, use the partner dashboard to go available when you are ready to receive delivery opportunities.

steps.title: Claim delivery orders
steps.body: View available pickups, claim one order at a time, mark pickup, update on-the-way status, and confirm delivery.

partnerRestaurant.title: Partner Restaurant
partnerRestaurant.description: Bring your restaurant to T-Food with a storefront, menu controls, order workflow, analytics, payout visibility, and delivery readiness tools.

features.title: Build your storefront
features.body: Create your restaurant profile, add contact details, set delivery radius, pin pickup location, and upload a cover image.

features.title: Get verified
features.body: Upload an owner profile photo, one accepted identity document, and restaurant photos. Your storefront becomes public after operations approval.

features.title: Operate daily
features.body: Accept orders, manage item availability, monitor analytics, view notifications, and track payout status.

steps.title: Register as merchant
steps.body: Create a merchant account and complete your business profile.

steps.title: Upload owner photo
steps.body: Add a clear owner profile photo from the merchant verification panel so operations can identify the responsible account owner.

steps.title: Upload identity document
steps.body: Submit one accepted identity document such as a national ID, passport, or voter card.

steps.title: Upload restaurant photo
steps.body: Add restaurant photos so T-Food can review storefront readiness and marketplace trust.

steps.title: Wait for approval
steps.body: T-Food operations reviews your account and documents before public selling is enabled. Rejection reasons are shown clearly if anything needs correction.

steps.title: Create storefront
steps.body: Add your restaurant profile, address, service settings, operating hours, images, and pickup details.

steps.title: Add menu
steps.body: Create menu items, prices, options, availability, and preparation details from the merchant dashboard.

steps.title: Start receiving orders
steps.body: Once verified, customers can discover your restaurant and your team can manage orders from the dashboard.
